user_system_path: Repo hardening Batch 3 (#429): gate verbose auth tracing (emails / user IDs) behind a FOLDERA_DEBUG_AUTH flag via a shared authDebugLog helper; scrub PII from auth error/warn diagnostics; remove debug console.log in usefulness-gate test. No behavior change for users; prod logs stop leaking PII. NO paid API calls.
allowed_files_raw: Batch 3 touches lib/auth/** (auth-options, supabase-auth-user, new auth-debug helper), the two OAuth callback routes, and one test in lib/briefing/__tests__. Control plane + docs as usual.
forbidden_files_raw: No schema, dependency, or tsconfig changes in this batch.
required_local_proof: npm run gate:continuity && npm run build && npm run lint && npm run typecheck
required_browser_proof: N/A — logging-hygiene change; no user-facing path change. Sign-in flow behavior is unchanged (only log verbosity is gated).
acceptance_condition: Verbose auth traces (emails / user IDs) only emit when FOLDERA_DEBUG_AUTH=true; build + lint + typecheck + gate green; auth + usefulness-gate vitest green.
anti_regression_checks: no PII (email / user id) logged on the default (flag-off) auth path
stop_condition: Stop when Batch 3 is merged and the control plane is ready for Batch 4 (or rolled to between-rungs at session end).
next_command: Proceed to Batch 4 — config & docs hygiene (.env.example completeness + dead skip-branch / TODO cleanup).
